name: Color Grading Theory
description: Understand color relationships, color wheels, and grading philosophy to make intentional rather than accidental color decisions.

name: Composition and Framing
description: Apply compositional principles — rule of thirds, leading lines, negative space — to strengthen the visual impact of a frame.

name: Light and Exposure
description: Read and control light quality, direction, and exposure to create the intended mood and tonal range.

name: Visual Storytelling
description: Use sequencing, contrast, and narrative arc to make a series of photographs tell a coherent story.

name: Portrait Retouching Philosophy
description: Retouch portraits to enhance rather than erase — preserve identity, texture, and specificity using frequency separation and dodge-and-burn.

name: Zone System Processing
description: Apply Ansel Adams' Zone System as a post-processing discipline — map tonal zones intentionally before touching a single slider.

name: Decisive Moment Selection
description: Apply Cartier-Bresson's contact-sheet discipline to select the one frame where form, gesture, and meaning coincide.

name: Print Output Pipeline
description: Manage the full color-managed pipeline from calibrated display to final print — soft proof, ICC profiles, output sharpening.
